title: Toggle Switch
description:
Build a **Toggle Switch** component in React with TypeScript.

### Requirements

- Render a toggle button with `role="switch"`
- Start in the **off** state by default (`aria-checked="false"`)
- Clicking the switch toggles it between on and off
- Support a `defaultOn` prop to start in the on state
- Support an `onChange` callback that receives the new boolean value
- Set `aria-checked` to reflect the current state
- Apply the CSS class `toggle-switch__track--on` to the button when the switch is on

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Toggle button | `toggle` |
constraints:
- Use React functional components with hooks
- Do not use any external UI libraries
- The toggle must be keyboard accessible via the native button element
hints: Use the useState hook to track whether the switch is on or off.
React automatically converts boolean aria-checked to the correct string.
Apply the --on modifier class conditionally so the CSS transition animates the thumb.
